hero.subtitle: AI-powered SAT test management for tutoring centers. Upload PDFs, auto-parse questions, assign tests, and track student performance.

dashboard.title: Admin Home
dashboard.body: Top-level overview of the SAT platform. Admins land here daily to scan pending student approvals, teacher counts, module status, and shortcut links into core workflows.

modules.title: Modules
modules.body: Upload and manage every SAT practice module (PDF). After AI parsing, track status and question counts here, then push the module through the question-bank approval flow. Admin / content lead use only.

questions.title: Question Bank
questions.body: Central review queue for AI-parsed questions. Filter by needs-review or low-confidence, then approve or reject in bulk. Only approved questions are served to students in tests.

tests.title: Tests
tests.body: Create, publish, and close student tests, and track who is assigned and who has submitted. Each test is bound to a single approved module.

students.title: Students
students.body: Approve new self-registered students, manage approved/suspended rosters, and assign students to class groups. Students cannot sign in or take tests until approved here.

teachers.title: Teachers
teachers.body: Invite teachers by email and review who is on the platform along with their assigned classes. Invited teachers complete sign-up themselves via the email link.

admins.title: Admins
admins.body: Manage everyone with admin access. Super admins can invite or remove other admins; regular admins see the roster but cannot grant admin access. Use this list to keep track of who can change platform-wide settings.

classes.title: Class Groups
classes.body: Create and maintain class groups (by campus / grade), then add students into them. Class groups make it fast to assign a test to an entire class at once.

reports.title: Platform Reports
reports.body: Cross-center reporting — test completion rates, student performance trends, and module effectiveness. Used by admins for planning and resourcing (coming soon).

settings.title: Platform Settings
settings.body: Branding, notification preferences, AI-parse defaults, and account configuration. Admin-only (coming soon).

aiUsage.title: AI Usage
aiUsage.body: This month's AI parse calls, token usage, and estimated cost. Use this to monitor spend and judge whether AI parsing is paying off.

dashboard.title: Teacher Home
dashboard.body: Overview of every test assigned to your classes. Quickly see this week's submissions, students who need attention, and your active assignments.

teachingMode.title: Teaching Mode
teachingMode.body: Walk into class with two answers ready: which questions to review from your latest test, and which skill your class is weakest on right now. Build a quick practice set in one click.

tests.title: My Tests
tests.body: All tests an admin has assigned to you. Open a test to see student submissions, score distribution, and question-level analytics.

results.title: Student Results
results.body: Cross-test view of every student you teach with scores and time spent. Filter by test or class group, then export CSV for parent or peer discussions.

analysis.title: Question Analysis
analysis.body: Aggregated error rates across all your assigned tests. Find the questions and domains your class struggles with most, then prep the next class review around them.

help.title: Quick Start
help.body: Five-step walkthrough for new teachers. Skim this before your first class to know what to click, in what order.

settings.title: Teacher Settings
settings.body: Profile, notification preferences, and teaching options. Personalize your account here (coming soon).

dashboard.title: Student Home
dashboard.body: Shows your upcoming tests, profile completeness, and target score. Your first stop on every sign-in to see what's due and where you stand.

tests.title: My Tests
tests.body: All tests your teacher has currently assigned to you. Watch the due dates — click Start Test on any unstarted test to enter timed mode.

results.title: My Results
results.body: Review your submitted tests with score, correct count, and time spent. When your teacher allows, dive into the answer review to study your weak spots.

profile.title: Profile
profile.body: Your grade, school, class group, and target SAT score. The more complete your profile, the better your teacher can tailor instruction to you.

questions.unansweredWarning: {count} unanswered question(s). Unanswered questions will be marked incorrect.

teacherResults.title: Student Results
teacherResults.emptyBody: You have no tests yet. Build a Draft test in Teaching Mode, or ask your admin to assign one — student results show up here as soon as anyone submits.
teacherResults.subtitle: Cross-test consolidated view across all your tests.
teacherResults.noSubmissions: No submissions yet. Once students submit, results will appear here.

teacherAnalysis.title: Question Analysis
teacherAnalysis.emptyBody: You have no tests yet. Once a test is assigned (build one in Teaching Mode or wait for your admin) and students submit, cross-test question analytics will appear here.
teacherAnalysis.subtitleLong: Cross-test hardest-questions view across all your tests.
teacherAnalysis.noSubmissions: No submissions yet. Once students submit, hardest-question analytics will appear.

analyticsTable.noQuestionAttempts: No question attempts in the selected scope.
analyticsTable.noResults: No results match the current filters.

teacherHelp.title: Quick Start for Teachers
teacherHelp.intro: Welcome — these five steps cover the entire flow from setting up your class to reviewing student results. Each step links to the page you need.

step1.title: Confirm your profile and class
step1.body: Check your displayed name and time zone in Settings. If your admin sent you a class group, it should already be linked. If not, ask the admin to add you to the right class group before students join.

step2.title: Build a quick test in Teaching Mode
step2.body: Teaching Mode lets you assemble a practice test from any approved module in one click — pick a module, pick the questions, and a Draft test is created and pushed to the class. Use this for skill drills and warm-ups.

step3.title: Review and publish tests
step3.body: All tests assigned to you (whether you built them in Teaching Mode or your admin created them) live under My Tests. Set the time limit, due date, and confirm the assigned students before publishing — students cannot start until the test is Published.

step4.title: Track student submissions and per-question analytics
step4.body: Once students submit, open the test to see score distribution, who's done, who's late, and per-question correctness. Use Question Analysis to find the items your whole class missed and plan your next class around them.

step5.title: Export results for parent or admin reports
step5.body: On any test results page, click Export CSV to download a spreadsheet of every student's score, time spent, and per-question answers. Open it in Excel or Google Sheets to share with parents or the admin team.

teacherHelp.supportBody: If something is unclear or broken during your trial, message Barry directly with a screenshot — we are actively iterating based on teacher feedback this week.
